1→class TerminalManager
     2→  DATA_DIR = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR", "/data")

     7→  class Error < StandardError; end

     9→  # Opens a web terminal for the given sandbox.
    10→  # Returns the URL path to the WeTTY session.
    11→  def open(sandbox:)
    12→    raise Error, "Sandbox is not running" unless sandbox.status == "running"
    13→    raise Error, "Sandbox has no container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?
    14→
    15→    user = sandbox.user
    16→    container_name = wetty_container_name(sandbox)
    17→
    18→    # Idempotent: if WeTTY container already running, ensure config exists and return
    19→    if container_running?(container_name)
    20→      write_traefik_config(sandbox)
    21→      return wetty_url(sandbox)
    22→    end
    23→
    24→    pull_image
    25→    ensure_network
    26→    connect_sandbox_to_network(sandbox)
    27→
    28→    # Write Traefik config early so it has time to detect the new route

    30→    write_traefik_config(sandbox)
    31→
    32→    key_dir = generate_keypair(sandbox)
    33→    inject_pubkey(sandbox, key_dir)
    34→    create_wetty_container(sandbox: sandbox, user: user, key_dir: key_dir)
    35→
    36→    wetty_url(sandbox)
    37→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    38→    raise Error, "Failed to open terminal: #{e.message}"
    39→  rescue SystemCallError => e
    40→    raise Error, "Failed to open terminal: #{e.message}"
    41→  end
    42→
    43→  # Closes the web terminal for the given sandbox.
    44→  def close(sandbox:)
    45→    container_name = wetty_container_name(sandbox)
    46→
    47→    # Stop and remove WeTTY container
    48→    begin
    49→      container = Docker::Container.get(container_name)
    50→      container.stop(t: 3) rescue nil
    51→      container.delete(force: true)
    52→    rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    53→      # Already gone
    54→    end
    55→
    56→    # Delete Traefik config
    57→    delete_traefik_config(sandbox)
    58→
    59→    # Clean up key directory
    60→    key_dir = key_dir_path(sandbox)
    61→    FileUtils.rm_rf(key_dir)
    62→
    63→    # Best-effort: remove pubkey from sandbox authorized_keys
    64→    remove_pubkey(sandbox)
    65→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    66→    Rails.logger.error("TerminalManager: close failed for #{sandbox.full_name}: #{e.message}")
    67→  end

    74→    TCPSocket.new(container_name, 3000).close

class TerminalManager
  DATA_DIR = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR", "/data")
  WETTY_IMAGE = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_WETTY_IMAGE", "wettyoss/wetty:latest")
  NETWORK_NAME = "sandcastle-web"
  DYNAMIC_DIR = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "dynamic")

  class Error < StandardError; end

  # Opens a web terminal for the given sandbox.
  # Returns the URL path to the WeTTY session.
  def open(sandbox:)
    raise Error, "Sandbox is not running" unless sandbox.status == "running"
    raise Error, "Sandbox has no container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?

    user = sandbox.user
    container_name = wetty_container_name(sandbox)

    # Idempotent: if WeTTY container already running, ensure config exists and return
    if container_running?(container_name)
      write_traefik_config(sandbox)
      return wetty_url(sandbox)
    end

    pull_image
    ensure_network
    connect_sandbox_to_network(sandbox)

    # Write Traefik config early so it has time to detect the new route
    # while we set up keypairs and start the WeTTY container.
    write_traefik_config(sandbox)

    key_dir = generate_keypair(sandbox)
    inject_pubkey(sandbox, key_dir)
    create_wetty_container(sandbox: sandbox, user: user, key_dir: key_dir)

    wetty_url(sandbox)
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to open terminal: #{e.message}"
  rescue SystemCallError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to open terminal: #{e.message}"
  end

  # Closes the web terminal for the given sandbox.
  def close(sandbox:)
    container_name = wetty_container_name(sandbox)

    # Stop and remove WeTTY container
    begin
      container = Docker::Container.get(container_name)
      container.stop(t: 3) rescue nil
      container.delete(force: true)
    rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
      # Already gone
    end

    # Delete Traefik config
    delete_traefik_config(sandbox)

    # Clean up key directory
    key_dir = key_dir_path(sandbox)
    FileUtils.rm_rf(key_dir)

    # Best-effort: remove pubkey from sandbox authorized_keys
    remove_pubkey(sandbox)
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    Rails.logger.error("TerminalManager: close failed for #{sandbox.full_name}: #{e.message}")
  end

  # Returns true if the WeTTY container is running AND its HTTP server is ready.
  def active?(sandbox:)
    container_name = wetty_container_name(sandbox)
    return false unless container_running?(container_name)

    TCPSocket.new(container_name, 3000).close
    true
  rescue Errno::ECONNREFUSED, Errno::ETIMEDOUT, SocketError
    false
  end

  # Removes orphaned WeTTY containers whose sandbox no longer exists or is not running.
  def cleanup_orphaned
    Docker::Container.all(all: true).each do |container|
      name = container.info.dig("Names")&.first&.delete_prefix("/")
      next unless name&.start_with?("sc-wetty-")

      labels = container.info["Labels"] || {}
      sandbox_id = labels["sandcastle.sandbox_id"]&.to_i

      sandbox = sandbox_id ? Sandbox.find_by(id: sandbox_id) : nil
      should_remove = sandbox.nil? || sandbox.status != "running"

      if should_remove
        container.stop(t: 3) rescue nil
        container.delete(force: true)
        Rails.logger.info("TerminalManager: removed orphaned WeTTY container #{name}")

        # Clean up Traefik config and keys if we have a sandbox_id
        if sandbox_id
          config_path = File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "terminal-#{sandbox_id}.yml")
          File.delete(config_path) if File.exist?(config_path)
        end

        # Extract full_name from container name and validate format
        full_name = name.delete_prefix("sc-wetty-")
        unless full_name.match?(/\A[a-z][a-z0-9_-]+-[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*\z/)
          Rails.logger.warn("TerminalManager: skipping suspicious container name: #{name}")
          next
        end

        key_dir = File.join(DATA_DIR, "wetty", full_name)
        expected_parent = File.join(DATA_DIR, "wetty")
        unless File.expand_path(key_dir).start_with?("#{File.expand_path(expected_parent)}/")
          Rails.logger.warn("TerminalManager: path traversal attempt detected: #{key_dir}")
          next
        end

        FileUtils.rm_rf(key_dir) if Dir.exist?(key_dir)
      end
    end
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    Rails.logger.error("TerminalManager: orphan cleanup failed: #{e.message}")
  end

  private

  def wetty_container_name(sandbox)
    "sc-wetty-#{sandbox.full_name}"
  end

  def wetty_url(sandbox)
    "/terminal/#{sandbox.id}/wetty"
  end

  def key_dir_path(sandbox)
    File.join(DATA_DIR, "wetty", sandbox.full_name)
  end

  def container_running?(name)
    container = Docker::Container.get(name)
    container.json.dig("State", "Running") == true
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    false
  end

  def pull_image
    Docker::Image.get(WETTY_IMAGE)
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    Docker::Image.create("fromImage" => WETTY_IMAGE)
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
    raise Error, "Failed to pull #{WETTY_IMAGE} — check network connectivity"
  end

  def ensure_network
    Docker::Network.get(NETWORK_NAME)
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    Docker::Network.create(NETWORK_NAME, "Driver" => "bridge")
  end

  def connect_sandbox_to_network(sandbox)
    return unless sandbox.container_id.present?

    network = Docker::Network.get(NETWORK_NAME)
    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)

    networks = container.json.dig("NetworkSettings", "Networks") || {}
    return if networks.key?(NETWORK_NAME)

    network.connect(sandbox.container_id)
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    # Container no longer exists - sync job will fix the DB state
    raise Error, "Sandbox container not found. Please refresh and try again."
  end

  def generate_keypair(sandbox)
    key_dir = key_dir_path(sandbox)
    # Remove entire dir to avoid stale keys with bad permissions from prior sessions
    FileUtils.rm_rf(key_dir)
    FileUtils.mkdir_p(key_dir, mode: 0o700)

    key_path = File.join(key_dir, "key")

    system("ssh-keygen", "-t", "ed25519", "-f", key_path, "-N", "", "-q", "-C", "wetty-#{sandbox.full_name}",
      exception: true)

    # Ensure private key is only readable by owner
    File.chmod(0o600, key_path)

    key_dir
  end

  def inject_pubkey(sandbox, key_dir)
    pubkey = File.read(File.join(key_dir, "key.pub")).strip
    username = sandbox.user.name

    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)

    # Write pubkey via base64 to avoid any shell-injection risk.
    # The stdin: approach hangs because docker-api never sends EOF to cat.
    encoded = Base64.strict_encode64("#{pubkey}\n")
    container.exec([ "mkdir", "-p", "/home/#{username}/.ssh" ])
    container.exec([ "sh", "-c", "echo #{encoded} | base64 -d >> /home/#{username}/.ssh/authorized_keys" ])
    container.exec([ "chown", "-R", "#{username}:#{username}", "/home/#{username}/.ssh" ])
    container.exec([ "chmod", "600", "/home/#{username}/.ssh/authorized_keys" ])
  end

  def create_wetty_container(sandbox:, user:, key_dir:)
    container_name = wetty_container_name(sandbox)

    # Remove any existing container with this name
    begin
      old = Docker::Container.get(container_name)
      old.stop(t: 3) rescue nil
      old.delete(force: true)
    rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
      # No existing container
    end

    ssh_command = [
      "ssh", "-p", "22",
      "-o", "StrictHostKeyChecking=no",
      "-o", "UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null",
      "-o", "LogLevel=ERROR",
      "-i", "/etc/wetty/key",
      "#{user.name}@#{sandbox.full_name}",
      "-t", "tmux new-session -A -s main"
    ].join(" ")

    container = Docker::Container.create(
      "name" => container_name,
      "Image" => WETTY_IMAGE,
      "Env" => [
        "COMMAND=#{ssh_command}",
        "BASE=/terminal/#{sandbox.id}/wetty"
      ],
      "HostConfig" => {
        "NetworkMode" => NETWORK_NAME,
        "RestartPolicy" => { "Name" => "no" },
        "Memory" => 128 * 1024 * 1024, # 128MB
        "NanoCpus" => 500_000_000 # 0.5 CPU
      },
      "Labels" => {
        "sandcastle.sandbox_id" => sandbox.id.to_s,
        "sandcastle.role" => "wetty"
      }
    )

    container.start
    copy_key_to_container(container, key_dir)
    container
  end

  # Copy the SSH private key into the WeTTY container via exec + base64.
  # This avoids bind-mount path issues when Rails runs inside a container
  # with a Docker volume for /data (the host path doesn't match).
  def copy_key_to_container(container, key_dir)
    key_content = File.read(File.join(key_dir, "key"))
    encoded = Base64.strict_encode64(key_content)

    container.exec([ "mkdir", "-p", "/etc/wetty" ])
    container.exec([ "sh", "-c", "echo #{encoded} | base64 -d > /etc/wetty/key" ])
    container.exec([ "chmod", "600", "/etc/wetty/key" ])
  end

  def write_traefik_config(sandbox)
    FileUtils.mkdir_p(DYNAMIC_DIR)

    host = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_HOST", "localhost")
    id = sandbox.id
    container_name = wetty_container_name(sandbox)

    rule = if ENV["SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE"] == "selfsigned"
      "HostRegexp(`.+`) && PathPrefix(`/terminal/#{id}/wetty`)"
    else
      "Host(`#{host}`) && PathPrefix(`/terminal/#{id}/wetty`)"
    end

    config = {
      "http" => {
        "routers" => {
          "terminal-#{id}" => {
            "rule" => rule,
            "service" => "terminal-#{id}",
            "entryPoints" => [ "websecure" ],
            "tls" => tls_config,
            "middlewares" => [ "terminal-auth-#{id}" ],
            "priority" => 100
          }
        },
        "middlewares" => {
          "terminal-auth-#{id}" => {
            "forwardAuth" => {
              "address" => "http://sandcastle-web:80/terminal/auth",
              "trustForwardHeader" => true
            }
          }
        },
        "services" => {
          "terminal-#{id}" => {
            "loadBalancer" => {
              "servers" => [ { "url" => "http://#{container_name}:3000" } ]
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }

    File.write(File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "terminal-#{id}.yml"), config.to_yaml)
  end

  def tls_config
    if ENV["SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE"] == "selfsigned"
      {}
    else
      { "certResolver" => "letsencrypt" }
    end
  end

  def delete_traefik_config(sandbox)
    path = File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "terminal-#{sandbox.id}.yml")
    File.delete(path) if File.exist?(path)
  end

  def remove_pubkey(sandbox)
    return unless sandbox.container_id.present?

    begin
      container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
      username = sandbox.user.name
      # Match end-of-line to avoid substring collisions
      # (e.g. "wetty-user-foo" must not also remove "wetty-user-foobar").
      # Sandbox names are [a-z0-9_-] only, so safe for regex.
      marker = "wetty-#{sandbox.full_name}$"
      container.exec([
        "sh", "-c",
        "grep -v '#{marker}' /home/#{username}/.ssh/authorized_keys > /tmp/ak_clean && " \
        "mv /tmp/ak_clean /home/#{username}/.ssh/authorized_keys || true"
      ])
    rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError, Docker::Error::DockerError
      # Sandbox container gone or exec failed — best-effort
    end
  end
end

class VncManager
  DATA_DIR = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR", "/data")
  NOVNC_IMAGE = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_NOVNC_IMAGE", "gotget/novnc:latest")
  NETWORK_NAME = "sandcastle-web"
  DYNAMIC_DIR = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "dynamic")

  class Error < StandardError; end

  # Opens a web-based VNC session for the given sandbox.
  # Returns the URL path to the noVNC session.
  def open(sandbox:)
    raise Error, "Sandbox is not running" unless sandbox.status == "running"
    raise Error, "Sandbox has no container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?

    user = sandbox.user
    container_name = vnc_container_name(sandbox)

    # Idempotent: if noVNC container already running, return URL
    if container_running?(container_name)
      return vnc_url(sandbox)
    end

    pull_image
    ensure_network
    connect_sandbox_to_network(sandbox)

    # Write Traefik config early so it has time to detect the new route
    write_traefik_config(sandbox)

    create_novnc_container(sandbox: sandbox, user: user)

    vnc_url(sandbox)
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to open browser: #{e.message}"
  rescue SystemCallError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to open browser: #{e.message}"
  end

  # Closes the web VNC session for the given sandbox.
  def close(sandbox:)
    container_name = vnc_container_name(sandbox)

    # Stop and remove noVNC container
    begin
      container = Docker::Container.get(container_name)
      container.stop(t: 3) rescue nil
      container.delete(force: true)
    rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
      # Already gone
    end

    # Delete Traefik config
    delete_traefik_config(sandbox)
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    Rails.logger.error("VncManager: close failed for #{sandbox.full_name}: #{e.message}")
  end

  # Returns true if the noVNC container is running AND its HTTP server is ready.
  # The container_running? check alone is insufficient — the websockify process
  # may not have bound to port 6080 yet when Docker reports the container as running.
  def active?(sandbox:)
    container_name = vnc_container_name(sandbox)
    return false unless container_running?(container_name)

    TCPSocket.new(container_name, 6080).close
    true
  rescue Errno::ECONNREFUSED, Errno::ETIMEDOUT, SocketError
    false
  end

  # Removes orphaned noVNC containers whose sandbox no longer exists or is not running.
  def cleanup_orphaned
    Docker::Container.all(all: true).each do |container|
      name = container.info.dig("Names")&.first&.delete_prefix("/")
      next unless name&.start_with?("sc-vnc-")

      labels = container.info["Labels"] || {}
      sandbox_id = labels["sandcastle.sandbox_id"]&.to_i

      sandbox = sandbox_id ? Sandbox.find_by(id: sandbox_id) : nil
      should_remove = sandbox.nil? || sandbox.status != "running"

      if should_remove
        container.stop(t: 3) rescue nil
        container.delete(force: true)
        Rails.logger.info("VncManager: removed orphaned noVNC container #{name}")

        # Clean up Traefik config if we have a sandbox_id
        if sandbox_id
          config_path = File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "vnc-#{sandbox_id}.yml")
          File.delete(config_path) if File.exist?(config_path)
        end
      end
    end
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    Rails.logger.error("VncManager: orphan cleanup failed: #{e.message}")
  end

  private

  def vnc_container_name(sandbox)
    "sc-vnc-#{sandbox.full_name}"
  end

  def vnc_url(sandbox)
    id = sandbox.id
    # Strip prefix is /vnc/{id}, so all requests under /vnc/{id}/* are forwarded
    # to the noVNC container with the prefix removed. This keeps noVNC's relative
    # asset paths (package.json, core/*.js) resolving correctly within the prefix.
    # The ?path= tells noVNC where to connect the WebSocket; Traefik strips
    # /vnc/{id} leaving /websockify which websockify handles.
    "/vnc/#{id}/vnc.html?path=vnc/#{id}/websockify&autoconnect=true"
  end

  def container_running?(name)
    container = Docker::Container.get(name)
    container.json.dig("State", "Running") == true
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    false
  end

  def pull_image
    Docker::Image.get(NOVNC_IMAGE)
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    Docker::Image.create("fromImage" => NOVNC_IMAGE)
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
    raise Error, "Failed to pull #{NOVNC_IMAGE} — check network connectivity"
  end

  def ensure_network
    Docker::Network.get(NETWORK_NAME)
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    Docker::Network.create(NETWORK_NAME, "Driver" => "bridge")
  end

  def connect_sandbox_to_network(sandbox)
    return unless sandbox.container_id.present?

    network = Docker::Network.get(NETWORK_NAME)
    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)

    networks = container.json.dig("NetworkSettings", "Networks") || {}
    return if networks.key?(NETWORK_NAME)

    network.connect(sandbox.container_id)
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    # Container no longer exists - sync job will fix the DB state
    raise Error, "Sandbox container not found. Please refresh and try again."
  end

  def create_novnc_container(sandbox:, user:)
    container_name = vnc_container_name(sandbox)

    # Remove any existing container with this name
    begin
      old = Docker::Container.get(container_name)
      old.stop(t: 3) rescue nil
      old.delete(force: true)
    rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
      # No existing container
    end

    host_config = {
      "NetworkMode" => NETWORK_NAME,
      "RestartPolicy" => { "Name" => "no" },
      "Memory" => 128 * 1024 * 1024, # 128MB — noVNC static files + websockify proxy
      "NanoCpus" => 250_000_000 # 0.25 CPU
    }

    # Run the noVNC image using its built-in launch.sh, which starts both the
    # mini-webserver (noVNC HTML/JS) and websockify (WS→RFB proxy) on port 6080.
    # Traefik routes everything under /vnc/{id} to this container; after
    # stripPrefix the websockify path becomes /websockify as noVNC expects.
    container = Docker::Container.create(
      "name" => container_name,
      "Image" => NOVNC_IMAGE,
      "Cmd" => [ "--vnc", "#{sandbox.full_name}:5900", "--listen", "6080" ],
      "HostConfig" => host_config,
      "Labels" => {
        "sandcastle.sandbox_id" => sandbox.id.to_s,
        "sandcastle.role" => "novnc"
      }
    )

    container.start
    container
  end

  def write_traefik_config(sandbox)
    FileUtils.mkdir_p(DYNAMIC_DIR)

    host = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_HOST", "localhost")
    id = sandbox.id
    container_name = vnc_container_name(sandbox)
    sandbox_name   = sandbox.full_name

    base_rule = if ENV["SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE"] == "selfsigned"
      "HostRegexp(`.+`) && PathPrefix(`/vnc/#{id}`)"
    else
      "Host(`#{host}`) && PathPrefix(`/vnc/#{id}`)"
    end

    # Single router: all traffic under /vnc/{id} goes to the noVNC sidecar.
    # The noVNC container (gotget/novnc launch.sh) serves static HTML/JS on /
    # and proxies WebSocket VNC frames via websockify on /websockify, both on
    # port 6080. stripPrefix removes /vnc/{id} before forwarding, so noVNC's
    # relative asset paths (package.json, core/*.js, /websockify) resolve correctly.
    config = {
      "http" => {
        "routers" => {
          "vnc-#{id}" => {
            "rule" => base_rule,
            "service" => "vnc-#{id}",
            "entryPoints" => [ "websecure" ],
            "tls" => tls_config,
            "middlewares" => [ "vnc-auth-#{id}", "vnc-stripprefix-#{id}" ],
            "priority" => 100
          }
        },
        "middlewares" => {
          "vnc-auth-#{id}" => {
            "forwardAuth" => {
              "address" => "http://sandcastle-web:80/vnc/auth",
              "trustForwardHeader" => true
            }
          },
          "vnc-stripprefix-#{id}" => {
            "stripPrefix" => {
              "prefixes" => [ "/vnc/#{id}" ]
            }
          }
        },
        "services" => {
          "vnc-#{id}" => {
            "loadBalancer" => {
              "servers" => [ { "url" => "http://#{container_name}:6080" } ]
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }

    File.write(File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "vnc-#{id}.yml"), config.to_yaml)
  end

  def tls_config
    if ENV["SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE"] == "selfsigned"
      {}
    else
      { "certResolver" => "letsencrypt" }
    end
  end

  def delete_traefik_config(sandbox)
    path = File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "vnc-#{sandbox.id}.yml")
    File.delete(path) if File.exist?(path)
  end
end

Now let me look at the controller to see where the delays might be. Neither `terminal_manager.rb` nor `vnc_manager.rb` has a `sleep` call. The delay must be in the controller - the `wait` page polls the `status` endpoint, and the status endpoint checks if the container is `active?`. 

From the log:
- Terminal POST completed in **922ms** — this seems like a lot for just Docker operations. But there's no explicit sleep.
- VNC POST completed in **405ms**

The user said there's a "2 sec delay". Let me look at the TerminalController and VncController to understand the wait/status flow. The wait page polls `/status` every 1 second, and when status is `ready`, it redirects. So the "2 sec delay" might be:


1. The polling interval on the wait page (1s poll interval)
2. The delay between the container starting and `active?` returning true

Looking at the logs more carefully, the Terminal POST at 17:27:55 completes at 17:27:56.429, but the first terminal auth request doesn't start until 17:27:58.529 — that's about 3 seconds total from POST to the actual auth requests. I need to check the controller code to see what's happening during that gap.

The 2-second delay the user mentioned might actually be coming from the wait page's polling mechanism rather than the service itself. If the page polls every second and needs 1-2 cycles to detect the container is ready, that alone could account for 1-2 seconds of the delay. Or there could be a hardcoded sleep somewhere in the controller logic. Let me examine the terminal and VNC controllers to find where the actual bottleneck is.

class TerminalController < ApplicationController
  allow_unauthenticated_access only: :auth
  skip_before_action :require_password_change, only: :auth
  skip_forgery_protection only: :open

  def open
    sandbox = find_sandbox

    TerminalManager.new.open(sandbox: sandbox)
    redirect_to terminal_wait_sandbox_path(sandbox), status: :see_other
  rescue TerminalManager::Error => e
    redirect_to root_path, alert: e.message
  end

  def wait
    @sandbox = find_sandbox
    @terminal_url = terminal_redirect_url("/terminal/#{@sandbox.id}/wetty")
  end

  def status
    sandbox = find_sandbox
    ready = TerminalManager.new.active?(sandbox: sandbox)
    render json: { status: ready ? "ready" : "waiting" }
  end

  def close
    sandbox = find_sandbox

    TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
    redirect_to root_path, notice: "Terminal closed"
  rescue TerminalManager::Error => e
    redirect_to root_path, alert: e.message
  end

  private

  def find_sandbox
    if Current.user.admin?
      Sandbox.active.find(params[:id])
    else
      Current.user.sandboxes.active.find(params[:id])
    end
  end

  # Build the full terminal URL. In production, Traefik is the entry point
  # so a relative path works. In local dev (selfsigned TLS), Rails may be
  # accessed directly on a different port, so we need an absolute URL.
  def terminal_redirect_url(path)
    base = ENV["SANDCASTLE_TERMINAL_URL"]
    base ? "#{base}#{path}" : path
  end

  public

  # Called by Traefik forwardAuth. Returns 200 to allow, or redirects to
  # login (which Traefik passes through to the browser).
  def auth
    forwarded_uri = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Uri"] || ""
    match = forwarded_uri.match(%r{/terminal/(\d+)/wetty})
    head(:unauthorized) and return unless match

    session_record = find_session_by_cookie
    unless session_record
      # Build the original terminal URL from Traefik's forwarded headers
      # so the user returns here after logging in.
      proto = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Proto"] || "https"
      host  = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Host"] || request.host_with_port
      original_url = "#{proto}://#{host}#{forwarded_uri}"
      session[:return_to_after_authenticating] = original_url
      redirect_to new_session_url, allow_other_host: true
      return
    end

    user = session_record.user
    sandbox = Sandbox.active.find_by(id: match[1].to_i)
    head(:unauthorized) and return unless sandbox && (sandbox.user_id == user.id || user.admin?)

    head :ok
  end
end

class VncController < ApplicationController
  allow_unauthenticated_access only: :auth
  skip_before_action :require_password_change, only: :auth
  skip_forgery_protection only: :open

  def open
    sandbox = find_sandbox

    VncManager.new.open(sandbox: sandbox)
    redirect_to vnc_wait_sandbox_path(sandbox), status: :see_other
  rescue VncManager::Error => e
    redirect_to root_path, alert: e.message
  end

  def wait
    @sandbox = find_sandbox
    id = @sandbox.id
    @vnc_url = vnc_redirect_url("/vnc/#{id}/vnc.html?path=vnc/#{id}/websockify&autoconnect=true")
  end

  def status
    sandbox = find_sandbox
    ready = VncManager.new.active?(sandbox: sandbox)
    render json: { status: ready ? "ready" : "waiting" }
  end

  def close
    sandbox = find_sandbox

    VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
    redirect_to root_path, notice: "Browser session closed"
  rescue VncManager::Error => e
    redirect_to root_path, alert: e.message
  end

  private

  def find_sandbox
    if Current.user.admin?
      Sandbox.active.find(params[:id])
    else
      Current.user.sandboxes.active.find(params[:id])
    end
  end

  # Build the full VNC URL. In production, Traefik is the entry point
  # so a relative path works. In local dev (selfsigned TLS), Rails may be
  # accessed directly on a different port, so we need an absolute URL.
  def vnc_redirect_url(path)
    base = ENV["SANDCASTLE_VNC_URL"] || ENV["SANDCASTLE_TERMINAL_URL"]
    base ? "#{base}#{path}" : path
  end

  public

  # Called by Traefik forwardAuth. Returns 200 to allow, or redirects to
  # login (which Traefik passes through to the browser).
  def auth
    forwarded_uri = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Uri"] || ""
    match = forwarded_uri.match(%r{/vnc/(\d+)})
    head(:unauthorized) and return unless match

    session_record = find_session_by_cookie
    unless session_record
      # Build the original VNC URL from Traefik's forwarded headers
      # so the user returns here after logging in.
      proto = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Proto"] || "https"
      host  = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Host"] || request.host_with_port
      original_url = "#{proto}://#{host}#{forwarded_uri}"
      session[:return_to_after_authenticating] = original_url
      redirect_to new_session_url, allow_other_host: true
      return
    end

    user = session_record.user
    sandbox = Sandbox.active.find_by(id: match[1].to_i)
    head(:unauthorized) and return unless sandbox && (sandbox.user_id == user.id || user.admin?)

    head :ok
  end
end

content.thinking:
Now I understand the issue clearly. The polling interval in both `terminal/wait.html.erb` and `vnc/wait.html.erb` is `1000ms` (1 second). The `setInterval` fires every second.

But from the logs, I can see:
- Terminal POST at `17:27:55.507` (Traefik received), completed at `17:27:56.429` (Rails completed 303 in 922ms), Traefik done at `17:27:56.429` with 1554ms total
- First status poll at `17:27:57.525` → responded 200 with `{"status":"waiting"}` (18 chars, "waiting")


- Second status poll at `17:27:58.529` → responded 200 with `{"status":"ready"}` (18 chars), so the container became ready between the first and second check, triggering the redirect.

Looking at the timing more carefully, the terminal POST happened at 17:27:55, then status polling started around 17:27:57-58, with the redirect occurring after the "ready" response. That's roughly a 2-3 second wait before the terminal auth requests flooded in.

The VNC flow was much faster though—POST at 17:28:03, the `/wait` endpoint completed almost immediately at 17:28:03.634, and then status checks started at 17:28:04, so the VNC container was ready within about a second.

The real bottleneck is that the polling interval is set to 1000ms but doesn't start until after the page loads, creating an inherent 1-2 second delay before the first poll even fires. If the container isn't ready on that first attempt, you're waiting another full second for the next check. Dropping the poll interval to 250ms or 100ms would cut that initial delay significantly and make subsequent retries much faster. WeTTY container `active?` check (TCPSocket to port 3000) usually succeeds within ~1 second of the container starting, and noVNC similarly checks port 6080. Since the first poll fires after 1 second, we miss the ready state and wait for the second poll—reducing the interval to ~250ms would catch it much sooner. Looking at the timing: the `open` POST takes ~922ms, then we redirect to `/wait` where the first poll at T+1s finds the container likely already ready. With a 250ms interval, we'd poll at T+250ms (T+1.2s from the original start) when WeTTY is almost certainly ready.

The real improvement though is polling immediately on page load instead of waiting for the first interval to fire, then using setInterval for subsequent checks.

Implementing the polling logic... I'm looking at the timing from the logs—the container starts quickly after the POST request, and the page loads almost immediately, so an initial poll should fire right away rather than waiting. I'll simplify this by calling `check()` once upfront, then setting the interval to 250ms, and adjusting the timeout counter to account for the faster polling rate by multiplying it by 4.

Actually, a cleaner approach is to track wall time instead—I'll capture the start time and check if 30 seconds have elapsed rather than counting polls, which makes the timeout logic independent of the interval frequency.

content.text:
Found it. The polling interval is **1000ms** in both wait pages, and crucially `setInterval` doesn't fire immediately — so the first check is always at least 1 second after page load. By that point if the container isn't ready, it's another full second before the next check. That's your 2-second delay.

**The fix:** poll immediately on load + use 250ms interval + use wall-clock timeout. Let me apply it to both views:

input.old_string:
<script>
  (function() {
    var statusUrl = "<%= terminal_status_sandbox_path(@sandbox) %>";
    var terminalUrl = "<%= j @terminal_url %>";
    var elapsed = 0;
    var timeout = 30;

    var poll = setInterval(function() {
      elapsed++;

      if (elapsed > timeout) {
        clearInterval(poll);
        document.getElementById("connecting").classList.add("hidden");
        document.getElementById("timeout-error").classList.remove("hidden");
        return;
      }

      fetch(statusUrl, { headers: { "Accept": "application/json" } })
        .then(function(r) { return r.json(); })
        .then(function(data) {
          if (data.status === "ready") {
            clearInterval(poll);
            window.location.href = terminalUrl;
          }
        })
        .catch(function() {});
    }, 1000);
  })();
</script>
input.new_string:
<script>
  (function() {
    var statusUrl = "<%= terminal_status_sandbox_path(@sandbox) %>";
    var terminalUrl = "<%= j @terminal_url %>";
    var startTime = Date.now();
    var TIMEOUT_MS = 30000;
    var poll;

    function check() {
      if (Date.now() - startTime > TIMEOUT_MS) {
        clearInterval(poll);
        document.getElementById("connecting").classList.add("hidden");
        document.getElementById("timeout-error").classList.remove("hidden");
        return;
      }

      fetch(statusUrl, { headers: { "Accept": "application/json" } })
        .then(function(r) { return r.json(); })
        .then(function(data) {
          if (data.status === "ready") {
            clearInterval(poll);
            window.location.href = terminalUrl;
          }
        })
        .catch(function() {});
    }

    check();
    poll = setInterval(check, 250);
  })();
</script>

content.text:
Deployed. Here's what I found and fixed:

**Root cause:** Both wait pages used `setInterval(..., 1000)` — which means the **first status check fires 1 second after page load**. If the container isn't ready in that exact window, you wait another full second for the next check. Hence the consistent ~2s delay.

**Fix applied to both `terminal/wait.html.erb` and `vnc/wait.html.erb`:**
- `check()` fires **immediately** on page load (no initial wait)
- Then polls every **250ms** instead of 1000ms
- Timeout uses wall clock (`Date.now()`) instead of a counter — still 30s

Try opening a terminal and VNC again — you should see it connect in ~250ms of the container being ready rather than the next 1s tick.

message.content: i expect the waiting page to be avain immediately!

content.thinking:
The user wants the wait page to be skipped entirely if the container is already ready. Looking at the flow:

1. `open` is called → creates the container → redirects to `/wait`
2. The wait page polls `/status` 

But the container is often ALREADY ready by the time the wait page loads, since `open` synchronously creates and starts the container. So instead of redirecting to a wait page and then polling, we should check if it's already ready and redirect directly to the terminal/VNC URL.

The fix should be in the controller: after `open`, check if it's immediately active, and if so redirect directly to the terminal/VNC URL instead of to the wait page.


I'm looking at the terminal controller's `open` method, which calls `TerminalManager.new.open` to create and start the container. Since this happens synchronously, the container might already be ready by the time the method returns, so I should check the `active?` status right after and conditionally redirect either to the wait page or directly to the terminal URL.

However, I'm concerned about the performance impact of the `active?` check since it performs a TCP socket connection test. Let me examine what that method actually does to see if it's worth the latency trade-off.

The `active?` method first checks if the container is running via a Docker API call, then attempts to establish a TCP connection to port 3000. The issue is that `TCPSocket.new` without an explicit timeout could hang indefinitely if the container is still starting up and the port isn't responding yet. In a Docker network, DNS might resolve but the port could still be unavailable, causing the system to wait for the default timeout which could be quite long.

Looking at the timing in the logs, the container creation takes about 922ms, but the first status poll shortly after returns "waiting" — the container isn't immediately ready when the creation completes. The subsequent polls then show it transitioning to "ready" a few hundred milliseconds later.

The user probably expects the wait page to not appear at all if the container becomes ready fast enough. So maybe the approach is to poll `active?` briefly right after creating the container in the `open` action, and if it's ready within that window, skip the wait page entirely and redirect straight to the URL. Otherwise, fall back to showing the wait page as usual.

Looking at the timing from the logs, the Rails request takes about 900ms to create and start the container, but WeTTY might not be immediately responsive after that. So I could add a short readiness check in the controller's `open` action itself...

Writing the readiness check logic... I see the optimization now—when the container's already running, `active?` will return true immediately and we skip the wait page entirely. But for a fresh container that just started, WeTTY needs time to bind to the port, so `active?` returns false and we show the wait page. The real goal is simpler though: just avoid the wait page if the terminal responds fast enough. the real solution is to check if the terminal is already active right in the `open` action itself—if it is, redirect straight to the terminal; if not, show the wait page instead. I'm modifying the controller to call `active?` after opening and branch accordingly, avoiding the wait page flash entirely for the idempotent case. I need to check how the `wait` action constructs the terminal URL so I can use the same approach in the `open` action for a direct redirect. Looking at the potential issue with `active?` — when a container is freshly started, the TCP socket check might hang due to system timeouts, though it does catch connection refused errors immediately. The timeout handling needs to account for this delay.

For the flow: if the container's already running, `active?` connects successfully and we redirect straight to the terminal; if it's freshly started, `active?` gets refused immediately and we redirect to the wait page, which polls every 250ms until the connection succeeds.

The main risk is ETIMEDOUT from network issues, but in Docker bridge networking, an unbound port typically returns ECONNREFUSED quickly rather than hanging, so this approach should be safe.

content.text: Right — the wait page itself is the problem. We should check readiness right in the `open` action and only show the wait page if actually needed. If the container is already up (re-open) or comes up fast enough, go straight there.

class TerminalController < ApplicationController
  allow_unauthenticated_access only: :auth
  skip_before_action :require_password_change, only: :auth
  skip_forgery_protection only: :open

  def open
    sandbox = find_sandbox

    TerminalManager.new.open(sandbox: sandbox)
    redirect_to terminal_wait_sandbox_path(sandbox), status: :see_other
  rescue TerminalManager::Error => e
    redirect_to root_path, alert: e.message
  end

  def wait
    @sandbox = find_sandbox
    @terminal_url = terminal_redirect_url("/terminal/#{@sandbox.id}/wetty")
  end

  def status
    sandbox = find_sandbox
    ready = TerminalManager.new.active?(sandbox: sandbox)
    render json: { status: ready ? "ready" : "waiting" }
  end

  def close
    sandbox = find_sandbox

    TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
    redirect_to root_path, notice: "Terminal closed"
  rescue TerminalManager::Error => e
    redirect_to root_path, alert: e.message
  end

  private

  def find_sandbox
    if Current.user.admin?
      Sandbox.active.find(params[:id])
    else
      Current.user.sandboxes.active.find(params[:id])
    end
  end

  # Build the full terminal URL. In production, Traefik is the entry point
  # so a relative path works. In local dev (selfsigned TLS), Rails may be
  # accessed directly on a different port, so we need an absolute URL.
  def terminal_redirect_url(path)
    base = ENV["SANDCASTLE_TERMINAL_URL"]
    base ? "#{base}#{path}" : path
  end

  public

  # Called by Traefik forwardAuth. Returns 200 to allow, or redirects to
  # login (which Traefik passes through to the browser).
  def auth
    forwarded_uri = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Uri"] || ""
    match = forwarded_uri.match(%r{/terminal/(\d+)/wetty})
    head(:unauthorized) and return unless match

    session_record = find_session_by_cookie
    unless session_record
      # Build the original terminal URL from Traefik's forwarded headers
      # so the user returns here after logging in.
      proto = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Proto"] || "https"
      host  = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Host"] || request.host_with_port
      original_url = "#{proto}://#{host}#{forwarded_uri}"
      session[:return_to_after_authenticating] = original_url
      redirect_to new_session_url, allow_other_host: true
      return
    end

    user = session_record.user
    sandbox = Sandbox.active.find_by(id: match[1].to_i)
    head(:unauthorized) and return unless sandbox && (sandbox.user_id == user.id || user.admin?)

    head :ok
  end
end

class VncController < ApplicationController
  allow_unauthenticated_access only: :auth
  skip_before_action :require_password_change, only: :auth
  skip_forgery_protection only: :open

  def open
    sandbox = find_sandbox

    VncManager.new.open(sandbox: sandbox)
    redirect_to vnc_wait_sandbox_path(sandbox), status: :see_other
  rescue VncManager::Error => e
    redirect_to root_path, alert: e.message
  end

  def wait
    @sandbox = find_sandbox
    id = @sandbox.id
    @vnc_url = vnc_redirect_url("/vnc/#{id}/vnc.html?path=vnc/#{id}/websockify&autoconnect=true")
  end

  def status
    sandbox = find_sandbox
    ready = VncManager.new.active?(sandbox: sandbox)
    render json: { status: ready ? "ready" : "waiting" }
  end

  def close
    sandbox = find_sandbox

    VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
    redirect_to root_path, notice: "Browser session closed"
  rescue VncManager::Error => e
    redirect_to root_path, alert: e.message
  end

  private

  def find_sandbox
    if Current.user.admin?
      Sandbox.active.find(params[:id])
    else
      Current.user.sandboxes.active.find(params[:id])
    end
  end

  # Build the full VNC URL. In production, Traefik is the entry point
  # so a relative path works. In local dev (selfsigned TLS), Rails may be
  # accessed directly on a different port, so we need an absolute URL.
  def vnc_redirect_url(path)
    base = ENV["SANDCASTLE_VNC_URL"] || ENV["SANDCASTLE_TERMINAL_URL"]
    base ? "#{base}#{path}" : path
  end

  public

  # Called by Traefik forwardAuth. Returns 200 to allow, or redirects to
  # login (which Traefik passes through to the browser).
  def auth
    forwarded_uri = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Uri"] || ""
    match = forwarded_uri.match(%r{/vnc/(\d+)})
    head(:unauthorized) and return unless match

    session_record = find_session_by_cookie
    unless session_record
      # Build the original VNC URL from Traefik's forwarded headers
      # so the user returns here after logging in.
      proto = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Proto"] || "https"
      host  = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Host"] || request.host_with_port
      original_url = "#{proto}://#{host}#{forwarded_uri}"
      session[:return_to_after_authenticating] = original_url
      redirect_to new_session_url, allow_other_host: true
      return
    end

    user = session_record.user
    sandbox = Sandbox.active.find_by(id: match[1].to_i)
    head(:unauthorized) and return unless sandbox && (sandbox.user_id == user.id || user.admin?)

    head :ok
  end
end

content.text:
Done. The logic is now:

1. `open` creates/starts the container
2. Immediately checks `active?` (TCPSocket connect — fast, either connects or gets ECONNREFUSED instantly)
3. **If ready → redirect straight to the terminal/VNC URL** (no wait page at all)
4. **If not ready yet → show wait page** (which now polls at 250ms)

For the idempotent case (container already running) you'll go straight through. For a fresh container you'll only see the wait page for the ~250ms it takes WeTTY/noVNC to bind their port.

content.thinking:
Now I can see what's happening clearly. Let me analyze:

**Terminal (sandbox 24):**
- POST `/sandboxes/24/terminal` at `17:41:53` → still redirected to wait page (`17:41:54 Redirected to .../terminal/wait`)
- Wait page loaded at `17:41:54.375`
- Status polls:
  - `17:41:54.470` → 200 with 20 bytes = `{"status":"waiting"}` (resp_content_length: 20)
  - `17:41:54.718` → 304 Not Modified! (resp_content_length: 0)
  - `17:41:54.963` → 304 Not Modified!
  - `17:41:55.213` → 304 Not Modified!
  - `17:41:55.463` → 200 with 18 bytes = `{"status":"ready"}` (resp_content_length: 18)

The terminal went through about a second of polling before finally reporting ready status.

**VNC (sandbox 24):**
- POST `/sandboxes/24/vnc` at `17:41:58` → also hitting the wait page redirect
- Wait page loaded at `17:41:58.870`
- Status polls show the same pattern: mostly 304s until `17:41:59.942` returns ready

So VNC took roughly the same duration. But I'm seeing a critical issue here — the browser is caching these status responses, which means when it gets a 304 Not Modified, it's serving the stale "waiting" response from cache instead of recognizing that the status has actually changed to ready.

Looking at the content lengths, "waiting" is 20 bytes and "ready" is 18 bytes. The browser keeps returning the cached "waiting" response on 304s, so the JavaScript never sees the status change until the server response body actually differs enough that the cache is invalidated.

The real issue is that the status endpoint needs `no-cache` headers to prevent this HTTP caching behavior. I'm also noticing the `open` action redirects to the wait page even for existing containers, and the POST request timing shows about 1.3 seconds before the wait page appears, suggesting the `active?` check is returning false for the freshly started container. The root cause is that Rails automatically adds ETags to JSON responses, so when the browser polls the status endpoint, it caches the "waiting" response with an ETag. On subsequent requests, the browser sends `If-None-Match` with that ETag, and since the "waiting" response hasn't changed, Rails returns 304 Not Modified — which tells the browser to use its cached version, so it never sees the "ready" response even after the status changes.

The simplest fix is to add a cache-busting query parameter like a timestamp to each fetch request...

Actually, the proper server-side solution is cleaner: I'll add `Cache-Control: no-store` headers to the status actions so Rails never caches these responses in the first place.

But since we already patched the views with cache busters on the deployed container using `docker cp`, and a restart just restarts the process without recreating the container, those patches should still be there.

The real problem is the 304 responses from Rails' automatic ETag generation—the browser caches based on `If-None-Match` headers. I need to add a timestamp query parameter to the fetch URL to force fresh requests. But I'm also noticing that `active?` is returning false immediately in the `open` action for existing containers, which is why the wait page shows up instead of a direct redirect.

content.text:
Found the problem. **HTTP 304 caching.** The browser caches the `"waiting"` status response and keeps getting 304s instead of re-fetching — so it never sees `"ready"` until the response body actually changes. Look at the pattern:

```
17:41:54.470 → status 200 "waiting"  (cached)
17:41:54.718 → status 304            (browser uses cached "waiting")
17:41:54.963 → status 304            (browser uses cached "waiting")
17:41:55.213 → status 304            (browser uses cached "waiting")
17:41:55.463 → status 200 "ready"    (finally different content, cache miss)
```

Fix: disable caching on the status actions server-side:

toolUseResult.originalFile:
class TerminalController < ApplicationController
  allow_unauthenticated_access only: :auth
  skip_before_action :require_password_change, only: :auth
  skip_forgery_protection only: :open

  def open
    sandbox = find_sandbox
    manager = TerminalManager.new
    manager.open(sandbox: sandbox)

    if manager.active?(sandbox: sandbox)
      redirect_to terminal_redirect_url("/terminal/#{sandbox.id}/wetty"), allow_other_host: true, status: :see_other
    else
      redirect_to terminal_wait_sandbox_path(sandbox), status: :see_other
    end
  rescue TerminalManager::Error => e
    redirect_to root_path, alert: e.message
  end

  def wait
    @sandbox = find_sandbox
    @terminal_url = terminal_redirect_url("/terminal/#{@sandbox.id}/wetty")
  end

  def status
    sandbox = find_sandbox
    ready = TerminalManager.new.active?(sandbox: sandbox)
    render json: { status: ready ? "ready" : "waiting" }
  end

  def close
    sandbox = find_sandbox

    TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
    redirect_to root_path, notice: "Terminal closed"
  rescue TerminalManager::Error => e
    redirect_to root_path, alert: e.message
  end

  private

  def find_sandbox
    if Current.user.admin?
      Sandbox.active.find(params[:id])
    else
      Current.user.sandboxes.active.find(params[:id])
    end
  end

  # Build the full terminal URL. In production, Traefik is the entry point
  # so a relative path works. In local dev (selfsigned TLS), Rails may be
  # accessed directly on a different port, so we need an absolute URL.
  def terminal_redirect_url(path)
    base = ENV["SANDCASTLE_TERMINAL_URL"]
    base ? "#{base}#{path}" : path
  end

  public

  # Called by Traefik forwardAuth. Returns 200 to allow, or redirects to
  # login (which Traefik passes through to the browser).
  def auth
    forwarded_uri = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Uri"] || ""
    match = forwarded_uri.match(%r{/terminal/(\d+)/wetty})
    head(:unauthorized) and return unless match

    session_record = find_session_by_cookie
    unless session_record
      # Build the original terminal URL from Traefik's forwarded headers
      # so the user returns here after logging in.
      proto = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Proto"] || "https"
      host  = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Host"] || request.host_with_port
      original_url = "#{proto}://#{host}#{forwarded_uri}"
      session[:return_to_after_authenticating] = original_url
      redirect_to new_session_url, allow_other_host: true
      return
    end

    user = session_record.user
    sandbox = Sandbox.active.find_by(id: match[1].to_i)
    head(:unauthorized) and return unless sandbox && (sandbox.user_id == user.id || user.admin?)

    head :ok
  end
end

class VncController < ApplicationController
  allow_unauthenticated_access only: :auth
  skip_before_action :require_password_change, only: :auth
  skip_forgery_protection only: :open

  def open
    sandbox = find_sandbox
    manager = VncManager.new
    manager.open(sandbox: sandbox)

    if manager.active?(sandbox: sandbox)
      redirect_to vnc_redirect_url("/vnc/#{sandbox.id}/vnc.html?path=vnc/#{sandbox.id}/websockify&autoconnect=true"), allow_other_host: true, status: :see_other
    else
      redirect_to vnc_wait_sandbox_path(sandbox), status: :see_other
    end
  rescue VncManager::Error => e
    redirect_to root_path, alert: e.message
  end

  def wait
    @sandbox = find_sandbox
    id = @sandbox.id
    @vnc_url = vnc_redirect_url("/vnc/#{id}/vnc.html?path=vnc/#{id}/websockify&autoconnect=true")
  end

  def status
    sandbox = find_sandbox
    ready = VncManager.new.active?(sandbox: sandbox)
    render json: { status: ready ? "ready" : "waiting" }
  end

  def close
    sandbox = find_sandbox

    VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
    redirect_to root_path, notice: "Browser session closed"
  rescue VncManager::Error => e
    redirect_to root_path, alert: e.message
  end

  private

  def find_sandbox
    if Current.user.admin?
      Sandbox.active.find(params[:id])
    else
      Current.user.sandboxes.active.find(params[:id])
    end
  end

  # Build the full VNC URL. In production, Traefik is the entry point
  # so a relative path works. In local dev (selfsigned TLS), Rails may be
  # accessed directly on a different port, so we need an absolute URL.
  def vnc_redirect_url(path)
    base = ENV["SANDCASTLE_VNC_URL"] || ENV["SANDCASTLE_TERMINAL_URL"]
    base ? "#{base}#{path}" : path
  end

  public

  # Called by Traefik forwardAuth. Returns 200 to allow, or redirects to
  # login (which Traefik passes through to the browser).
  def auth
    forwarded_uri = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Uri"] || ""
    match = forwarded_uri.match(%r{/vnc/(\d+)})
    head(:unauthorized) and return unless match

    session_record = find_session_by_cookie
    unless session_record
      # Build the original VNC URL from Traefik's forwarded headers
      # so the user returns here after logging in.
      proto = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Proto"] || "https"
      host  = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Host"] || request.host_with_port
      original_url = "#{proto}://#{host}#{forwarded_uri}"
      session[:return_to_after_authenticating] = original_url
      redirect_to new_session_url, allow_other_host: true
      return
    end

    user = session_record.user
    sandbox = Sandbox.active.find_by(id: match[1].to_i)
    head(:unauthorized) and return unless sandbox && (sandbox.user_id == user.id || user.admin?)

    head :ok
  end
end

content.text: Done. The 304s were the real culprit — Rails auto-generates ETags for JSON responses, browser caches the `"waiting"` body, and subsequent polls get 304 (cached "waiting") until the body changes to `"ready"`. Adding `Cache-Control: no-store` forces every poll to be a fresh fetch. No restart needed — Rails picks up the file change on the next request.

end

class TerminalOpenJob < ApplicationJob
  queue_as :default

  def perform(sandbox_id:)
    sandbox = Sandbox.find(sandbox_id)
    return unless sandbox.status == "running"

    sandbox.start_job("opening_terminal")

    begin
      TerminalManager.new.open(sandbox: sandbox)
      sandbox.finish_job
    rescue => e
      Rails.logger.error("TerminalOpenJob failed: #{e.message}\n#{e.backtrace.join("\n")}")
      sandbox.fail_job("Failed to open terminal: #{e.message}")
      # Don't re-raise - terminal failures shouldn't retry
    end
  end
end

class TerminalController < ApplicationController
  allow_unauthenticated_access only: :auth
  skip_before_action :require_password_change, only: :auth
  skip_forgery_protection only: :open

  def open
    sandbox = find_sandbox
    manager = TerminalManager.new
    manager.open(sandbox: sandbox)

    if manager.active?(sandbox: sandbox)
      redirect_to terminal_redirect_url("/terminal/#{sandbox.id}/wetty"), allow_other_host: true, status: :see_other
    else
      redirect_to terminal_wait_sandbox_path(sandbox), status: :see_other
    end
  rescue TerminalManager::Error => e
    redirect_to root_path, alert: e.message
  end

  def wait
    @sandbox = find_sandbox
    @terminal_url = terminal_redirect_url("/terminal/#{@sandbox.id}/wetty")
  end

  def status
    sandbox = find_sandbox
    response.headers["Cache-Control"] = "no-store"
    ready = TerminalManager.new.active?(sandbox: sandbox)
    render json: { status: ready ? "ready" : "waiting" }
  end

  def close
    sandbox = find_sandbox

    TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
    redirect_to root_path, notice: "Terminal closed"
  rescue TerminalManager::Error => e
    redirect_to root_path, alert: e.message
  end

  private

  def find_sandbox
    if Current.user.admin?
      Sandbox.active.find(params[:id])
    else
      Current.user.sandboxes.active.find(params[:id])
    end
  end

  # Build the full terminal URL. In production, Traefik is the entry point
  # so a relative path works. In local dev (selfsigned TLS), Rails may be
  # accessed directly on a different port, so we need an absolute URL.
  def terminal_redirect_url(path)
    base = ENV["SANDCASTLE_TERMINAL_URL"]
    base ? "#{base}#{path}" : path
  end

  public

  # Called by Traefik forwardAuth. Returns 200 to allow, or redirects to
  # login (which Traefik passes through to the browser).
  def auth
    forwarded_uri = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Uri"] || ""
    match = forwarded_uri.match(%r{/terminal/(\d+)/wetty})
    head(:unauthorized) and return unless match

    session_record = find_session_by_cookie
    unless session_record
      # Build the original terminal URL from Traefik's forwarded headers
      # so the user returns here after logging in.
      proto = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Proto"] || "https"
      host  = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Host"] || request.host_with_port
      original_url = "#{proto}://#{host}#{forwarded_uri}"
      session[:return_to_after_authenticating] = original_url
      redirect_to new_session_url, allow_other_host: true
      return
    end

    user = session_record.user
    sandbox = Sandbox.active.find_by(id: match[1].to_i)
    head(:unauthorized) and return unless sandbox && (sandbox.user_id == user.id || user.admin?)

    head :ok
  end
end

class VncController < ApplicationController
  allow_unauthenticated_access only: :auth
  skip_before_action :require_password_change, only: :auth
  skip_forgery_protection only: :open

  def open
    sandbox = find_sandbox
    manager = VncManager.new
    manager.open(sandbox: sandbox)

    if manager.active?(sandbox: sandbox)
      redirect_to vnc_redirect_url("/vnc/#{sandbox.id}/vnc.html?path=vnc/#{sandbox.id}/websockify&autoconnect=true"), allow_other_host: true, status: :see_other
    else
      redirect_to vnc_wait_sandbox_path(sandbox), status: :see_other
    end
  rescue VncManager::Error => e
    redirect_to root_path, alert: e.message
  end

  def wait
    @sandbox = find_sandbox
    id = @sandbox.id
    @vnc_url = vnc_redirect_url("/vnc/#{id}/vnc.html?path=vnc/#{id}/websockify&autoconnect=true")
  end

  def status
    sandbox = find_sandbox
    response.headers["Cache-Control"] = "no-store"
    ready = VncManager.new.active?(sandbox: sandbox)
    render json: { status: ready ? "ready" : "waiting" }
  end

  def close
    sandbox = find_sandbox

    VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
    redirect_to root_path, notice: "Browser session closed"
  rescue VncManager::Error => e
    redirect_to root_path, alert: e.message
  end

  private

  def find_sandbox
    if Current.user.admin?
      Sandbox.active.find(params[:id])
    else
      Current.user.sandboxes.active.find(params[:id])
    end
  end

  # Build the full VNC URL. In production, Traefik is the entry point
  # so a relative path works. In local dev (selfsigned TLS), Rails may be
  # accessed directly on a different port, so we need an absolute URL.
  def vnc_redirect_url(path)
    base = ENV["SANDCASTLE_VNC_URL"] || ENV["SANDCASTLE_TERMINAL_URL"]
    base ? "#{base}#{path}" : path
  end

  public

  # Called by Traefik forwardAuth. Returns 200 to allow, or redirects to
  # login (which Traefik passes through to the browser).
  def auth
    forwarded_uri = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Uri"] || ""
    match = forwarded_uri.match(%r{/vnc/(\d+)})
    head(:unauthorized) and return unless match

    session_record = find_session_by_cookie
    unless session_record
      # Build the original VNC URL from Traefik's forwarded headers
      # so the user returns here after logging in.
      proto = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Proto"] || "https"
      host  = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Host"] || request.host_with_port
      original_url = "#{proto}://#{host}#{forwarded_uri}"
      session[:return_to_after_authenticating] = original_url
      redirect_to new_session_url, allow_other_host: true
      return
    end

    user = session_record.user
    sandbox = Sandbox.active.find_by(id: match[1].to_i)
    head(:unauthorized) and return unless sandbox && (sandbox.user_id == user.id || user.admin?)

    head :ok
  end
end

content.thinking:
Now I understand the full picture. The fix is:

1. **Terminal controller**: Instead of calling `manager.open(sandbox: sandbox)` synchronously, use `TerminalOpenJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: sandbox.id)` - but first check if already active
2. **VNC controller**: Same pattern, but need to create `VncOpenJob` first

For terminal:
```ruby
def open
  sandbox = find_sandbox
  manager = TerminalManager.new

  # Already up? Skip the wait page.
  if manager.active?(sandbox: sandbox)
    redirect_to terminal_redirect_url("/terminal/#{sandbox.id}/wetty"), allow_other_host: true, status: :see_other
    return
  end

  # Kick off in background and return immediately.
  TerminalOpenJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: sandbox.id)
  redirect_to terminal_wait_sandbox_path(sandbox), status: :see_other
rescue TerminalManager::Error => e
  redirect_to root_path, alert: e.message
end
```

For VNC, I need to create a similar background job that finds the sandbox and handles the opening logic asynchronously, with proper error handling and status checks.

Then the VNC controller will check if a connection is already active and redirect to the appropriate websocket URL with the right parameters for the VNC viewer.

If the container isn't ready yet, it enqueues the background job and redirects to a wait page that polls for readiness. The `active?` check is just a quick TCP socket connection attempt, so the whole response stays under 50ms instead of waiting for the full container startup. The polling page will detect when the container's ready within about 250ms of it actually becoming available.

Now let me put all three optimizations together.

file.content:
class TerminalOpenJob < ApplicationJob
  queue_as :default

  def perform(sandbox_id:)
    sandbox = Sandbox.find(sandbox_id)
    return unless sandbox.status == "running"

    sandbox.start_job("opening_terminal")

    begin
      TerminalManager.new.open(sandbox: sandbox)
      sandbox.finish_job
    rescue => e
      Rails.logger.error("TerminalOpenJob failed: #{e.message}\n#{e.backtrace.join("\n")}")
      sandbox.fail_job("Failed to open terminal: #{e.message}")
      # Don't re-raise - terminal failures shouldn't retry
    end
  end
end

input.content:
class VncOpenJob < ApplicationJob
  queue_as :default

  def perform(sandbox_id:)
    sandbox = Sandbox.find(sandbox_id)
    return unless sandbox.status == "running"

    sandbox.start_job("opening_vnc")

    begin
      VncManager.new.open(sandbox: sandbox)
      sandbox.finish_job
    rescue => e
      Rails.logger.error("VncOpenJob failed: #{e.message}\n#{e.backtrace.join("\n")}")
      sandbox.fail_job("Failed to open browser: #{e.message}")
      # Don't re-raise - VNC failures shouldn't retry
    end
  end
end

      Rails.logger.error("VncOpenJob failed: #{e.message}\n#{e.backtrace.join("\n")}")
      sandbox.fail_job("Failed to open browser: #{e.message}")
      # Don't re-raise - VNC failures shouldn't retry
    end
  end
end

file.content:
services:
  traefik:
    image: traefik:v3.6.8
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "80:80"
      - "443:443"
    volumes:
      - /data/traefik/traefik.yml:/etc/traefik/traefik.yml:ro
      - /data/traefik/dynamic:/data/dynamic:ro
      - /data/traefik/acme.json:/data/acme.json
      - /data/traefik/certs:/data/certs:ro
    networks:
      - sandcastle-web

  postgres:
    image: postgres:18
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - /data/postgres:/var/lib/postgresql
      - ./docker/postgres/init-databases.sh:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init-databases.sh:ro
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: sandcastle
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
      POSTGRES_DB: sandcastle_production
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U sandcastle -d sandcastle_production"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
    networks:
      - sandcastle-web

  web:
    image: ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle:latest
    container_name: sandcastle-web
    group_add:
      - "${DOCKER_GID:-988}"
    cap_add:
      - SYS_ADMIN
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      - /data:/data
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: production
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE}
      SANDCASTLE_HOST: ${SANDCASTLE_HOST}
      SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR: /data
      SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE: ${SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE:-letsencrypt}
      SANDCASTLE_ALT_HOSTNAMES: ${SANDCASTLE_ALT_HOSTNAMES:-}
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
      GITHUB_CLIENT_ID: ${GITHUB_CLIENT_ID:-}
      GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET: ${GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET:-}
      GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID: ${GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID:-}
      GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET: ${GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET:-}
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      migrate:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
    networks:
      - sandcastle-web

  worker:
    image: ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle:latest
    container_name: sandcastle-worker
    command: ["./bin/jobs"]
    group_add:
      - "${DOCKER_GID:-988}"
    cap_add:
      - SYS_ADMIN
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      - /data:/data
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: production
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE}
      SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR: /data
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
    restart: unless-stopped
    depends_on:
      migrate:
        condition: service_completed_successfully
    networks:
      - sandcastle-web

  migrate:
    image: ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle:latest
    command: ["./bin/rails", "db:prepare"]
    environment:
      RAILS_ENV: production
      SECRET_KEY_BASE: ${SECRET_KEY_BASE}
      DB_HOST: postgres
      DB_USER: sandcastle
      DB_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    networks:
      - sandcastle-web

networks:
  sandcastle-web:
    external: true

class TerminalManager
  DATA_DIR = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR", "/data")
  WETTY_IMAGE = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_WETTY_IMAGE", "wettyoss/wetty:latest")
  NETWORK_NAME = "sandcastle-web"
  DYNAMIC_DIR = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "dynamic")

  class Error < StandardError; end

  # Opens a web terminal for the given sandbox.
  # Returns the URL path to the WeTTY session.
  def open(sandbox:)
    raise Error, "Sandbox is not running" unless sandbox.status == "running"
    raise Error, "Sandbox has no container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?

    user = sandbox.user
    container_name = wetty_container_name(sandbox)

    # Idempotent: if WeTTY container already running, ensure config exists and return
    if container_running?(container_name)
      write_traefik_config(sandbox)
      return wetty_url(sandbox)
    end

    pull_image
    ensure_network
    connect_sandbox_to_network(sandbox)

    # Write Traefik config early so it has time to detect the new route
    # while we set up keypairs and start the WeTTY container.
    write_traefik_config(sandbox)

    key_dir = generate_keypair(sandbox)
    inject_pubkey(sandbox, key_dir)
    create_wetty_container(sandbox: sandbox, user: user, key_dir: key_dir)

    wetty_url(sandbox)
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to open terminal: #{e.message}"
  rescue SystemCallError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to open terminal: #{e.message}"
  end

  # Closes the web terminal for the given sandbox.
  def close(sandbox:)
    container_name = wetty_container_name(sandbox)

    # Stop and remove WeTTY container
    begin
      container = Docker::Container.get(container_name)
      container.stop(t: 3) rescue nil
      container.delete(force: true)
    rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
      # Already gone
    end

    # Delete Traefik config
    delete_traefik_config(sandbox)

    # Clean up key directory
    key_dir = key_dir_path(sandbox)
    FileUtils.rm_rf(key_dir)

    # Best-effort: remove pubkey from sandbox authorized_keys
    remove_pubkey(sandbox)
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    Rails.logger.error("TerminalManager: close failed for #{sandbox.full_name}: #{e.message}")
  end

  # Returns true if the WeTTY container is running AND its HTTP server is ready.
  def active?(sandbox:)
    container_name = wetty_container_name(sandbox)
    return false unless container_running?(container_name)

    TCPSocket.new(container_name, 3000).close
    true
  rescue Errno::ECONNREFUSED, Errno::ETIMEDOUT, SocketError
    false
  end

  # Removes orphaned WeTTY containers whose sandbox no longer exists or is not running.
  def cleanup_orphaned
    Docker::Container.all(all: true).each do |container|
      name = container.info.dig("Names")&.first&.delete_prefix("/")
      next unless name&.start_with?("sc-wetty-")

      labels = container.info["Labels"] || {}
      sandbox_id = labels["sandcastle.sandbox_id"]&.to_i

      sandbox = sandbox_id ? Sandbox.find_by(id: sandbox_id) : nil
      should_remove = sandbox.nil? || sandbox.status != "running"

      if should_remove
        container.stop(t: 3) rescue nil
        container.delete(force: true)
        Rails.logger.info("TerminalManager: removed orphaned WeTTY container #{name}")

        # Clean up Traefik config and keys if we have a sandbox_id
        if sandbox_id
          config_path = File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "terminal-#{sandbox_id}.yml")
          File.delete(config_path) if File.exist?(config_path)
        end

        # Extract full_name from container name and validate format
        full_name = name.delete_prefix("sc-wetty-")
        unless full_name.match?(/\A[a-z][a-z0-9_-]+-[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*\z/)
          Rails.logger.warn("TerminalManager: skipping suspicious container name: #{name}")
          next
        end

        key_dir = File.join(DATA_DIR, "wetty", full_name)
        expected_parent = File.join(DATA_DIR, "wetty")
        unless File.expand_path(key_dir).start_with?("#{File.expand_path(expected_parent)}/")
          Rails.logger.warn("TerminalManager: path traversal attempt detected: #{key_dir}")
          next
        end

        FileUtils.rm_rf(key_dir) if Dir.exist?(key_dir)
      end
    end
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    Rails.logger.error("TerminalManager: orphan cleanup failed: #{e.message}")
  end

  private

  def wetty_container_name(sandbox)
    "sc-wetty-#{sandbox.full_name}"
  end

  def wetty_url(sandbox)
    "/terminal/#{sandbox.id}/wetty"
  end

  def key_dir_path(sandbox)
    File.join(DATA_DIR, "wetty", sandbox.full_name)
  end

  def container_running?(name)
    container = Docker::Container.get(name)
    container.json.dig("State", "Running") == true
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    false
  end

  def pull_image
    Docker::Image.get(WETTY_IMAGE)
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    Docker::Image.create("fromImage" => WETTY_IMAGE)
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
    raise Error, "Failed to pull #{WETTY_IMAGE} — check network connectivity"
  end

  def ensure_network
    Docker::Network.get(NETWORK_NAME)
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    Docker::Network.create(NETWORK_NAME, "Driver" => "bridge")
  end

  def connect_sandbox_to_network(sandbox)
    return unless sandbox.container_id.present?

    network = Docker::Network.get(NETWORK_NAME)
    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)

    networks = container.json.dig("NetworkSettings", "Networks") || {}
    return if networks.key?(NETWORK_NAME)

    network.connect(sandbox.container_id)
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    # Container no longer exists - sync job will fix the DB state
    raise Error, "Sandbox container not found. Please refresh and try again."
  end

  def generate_keypair(sandbox)
    key_dir = key_dir_path(sandbox)
    # Remove entire dir to avoid stale keys with bad permissions from prior sessions
    FileUtils.rm_rf(key_dir)
    FileUtils.mkdir_p(key_dir, mode: 0o700)

    key_path = File.join(key_dir, "key")

    system("ssh-keygen", "-t", "ed25519", "-f", key_path, "-N", "", "-q", "-C", "wetty-#{sandbox.full_name}",
      exception: true)

    # Ensure private key is only readable by owner
    File.chmod(0o600, key_path)

    key_dir
  end

  def inject_pubkey(sandbox, key_dir)
    pubkey = File.read(File.join(key_dir, "key.pub")).strip
    username = sandbox.user.name

    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)

    # Write pubkey via base64 to avoid any shell-injection risk.
    # The stdin: approach hangs because docker-api never sends EOF to cat.
    encoded = Base64.strict_encode64("#{pubkey}\n")
    container.exec([ "mkdir", "-p", "/home/#{username}/.ssh" ])
    container.exec([ "sh", "-c", "echo #{encoded} | base64 -d >> /home/#{username}/.ssh/authorized_keys" ])
    container.exec([ "chown", "-R", "#{username}:#{username}", "/home/#{username}/.ssh" ])
    container.exec([ "chmod", "600", "/home/#{username}/.ssh/authorized_keys" ])
  end

  def create_wetty_container(sandbox:, user:, key_dir:)
    container_name = wetty_container_name(sandbox)

    # Remove any existing container with this name
    begin
      old = Docker::Container.get(container_name)
      old.stop(t: 3) rescue nil
      old.delete(force: true)
    rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
      # No existing container
    end

    ssh_command = [
      "ssh", "-p", "22",
      "-o", "StrictHostKeyChecking=no",
      "-o", "UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null",
      "-o", "LogLevel=ERROR",
      "-i", "/etc/wetty/key",
      "#{user.name}@#{sandbox.full_name}",
      "-t", "tmux new-session -A -s main"
    ].join(" ")

    container = Docker::Container.create(
      "name" => container_name,
      "Image" => WETTY_IMAGE,
      "Env" => [
        "COMMAND=#{ssh_command}",
        "BASE=/terminal/#{sandbox.id}/wetty"
      ],
      "HostConfig" => {
        "NetworkMode" => NETWORK_NAME,
        "RestartPolicy" => { "Name" => "no" },
        "Memory" => 128 * 1024 * 1024, # 128MB
        "NanoCpus" => 500_000_000 # 0.5 CPU
      },
      "Labels" => {
        "sandcastle.sandbox_id" => sandbox.id.to_s,
        "sandcastle.role" => "wetty"
      }
    )

    container.start
    copy_key_to_container(container, key_dir)
    container
  end

  # Copy the SSH private key into the WeTTY container via exec + base64.
  # This avoids bind-mount path issues when Rails runs inside a container
  # with a Docker volume for /data (the host path doesn't match).
  def copy_key_to_container(container, key_dir)
    key_content = File.read(File.join(key_dir, "key"))
    encoded = Base64.strict_encode64(key_content)

    container.exec([ "mkdir", "-p", "/etc/wetty" ])
    container.exec([ "sh", "-c", "echo #{encoded} | base64 -d > /etc/wetty/key" ])
    container.exec([ "chmod", "600", "/etc/wetty/key" ])
  end

  def write_traefik_config(sandbox)
    FileUtils.mkdir_p(DYNAMIC_DIR)

    host = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_HOST", "localhost")
    id = sandbox.id
    container_name = wetty_container_name(sandbox)

    rule = if ENV["SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE"] == "selfsigned"
      "HostRegexp(`.+`) && PathPrefix(`/terminal/#{id}/wetty`)"
    else
      "Host(`#{host}`) && PathPrefix(`/terminal/#{id}/wetty`)"
    end

    config = {
      "http" => {
        "routers" => {
          "terminal-#{id}" => {
            "rule" => rule,
            "service" => "terminal-#{id}",
            "entryPoints" => [ "websecure" ],
            "tls" => tls_config,
            "middlewares" => [ "terminal-auth-#{id}" ],
            "priority" => 100
          }
        },
        "middlewares" => {
          "terminal-auth-#{id}" => {
            "forwardAuth" => {
              "address" => "http://sandcastle-web:80/terminal/auth",
              "trustForwardHeader" => true
            }
          }
        },
        "services" => {
          "terminal-#{id}" => {
            "loadBalancer" => {
              "servers" => [ { "url" => "http://#{container_name}:3000" } ]
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }

    File.write(File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "terminal-#{id}.yml"), config.to_yaml)
  end

  def tls_config
    if ENV["SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE"] == "selfsigned"
      {}
    else
      { "certResolver" => "letsencrypt" }
    end
  end

  def delete_traefik_config(sandbox)
    path = File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "terminal-#{sandbox.id}.yml")
    File.delete(path) if File.exist?(path)
  end

  def remove_pubkey(sandbox)
    return unless sandbox.container_id.present?

    begin
      container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
      username = sandbox.user.name
      # Match end-of-line to avoid substring collisions
      # (e.g. "wetty-user-foo" must not also remove "wetty-user-foobar").
      # Sandbox names are [a-z0-9_-] only, so safe for regex.
      marker = "wetty-#{sandbox.full_name}$"
      container.exec([
        "sh", "-c",
        "grep -v '#{marker}' /home/#{username}/.ssh/authorized_keys > /tmp/ak_clean && " \
        "mv /tmp/ak_clean /home/#{username}/.ssh/authorized_keys || true"
      ])
    rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError, Docker::Error::DockerError
      # Sandbox container gone or exec failed — best-effort
    end
  end
end
